LAW AND MOTION 302, TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER AND ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. PLAINTIFF THERMO ENERGY SYSTEMS, INC.'S MOTION FOR A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION ENJOINING DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A FROM DISPOSING IN ANY WAY WITH THE MONEY DEPOSITED INTO WELLS FARGO BANK ACCOUNTS XXXXXX4046 AND XXXXXX4826 UNTIL ENTRY OF FINAL JUDGMENT IN THIS ACTION IS GRANTED. THE MOTION IS UNOPPOSED AND THERMO ENERGY HAS SHOWN THAT IT IS LIKELY TO PREVAIL ON THE MERITS OF ITS FRAUD CLAIMS AGAINST DOE DEFENDANTS BASED ON EVIDENCE THAT THERMO ENERGY HAS BEEN THE VICTIM OF A FRAUDULENT PHISHING SCHEME. (COLLARD DECL. EXH. A-E 12). (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS) PER CCP 529 THERMO ENERGY IS REQUIRED TO POST A BOND IN THE AMOUNT OF $10,000.00. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S TRANSCRIPT) ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-23-2019 Off Calendar

Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Feb-11-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Dominic Signorotti, Esq. 9254-944-9700 of Buchman Provine Brothers Smith LLP for Plaintiff.
Sam Melamed, Esq. 415-398-3344 of Severson & Werson for Defendant WElls Fargo.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. Counsel for Wells Fargo informs the Court he is not contesting the tentative ruling. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction.

Plaintiff Thermo Energy Systems, Inc.'s motion for a preliminary injunction enjoining defendant Wells Fargo Bank, N.A from disposing in any way with the money deposited into Wells Fargo Bank accounts xxxxxx4046 and xxxxxx4826 until entry of final judgment in this action is granted. The motion is unopposed and Thermo Energy has shown that it is likely to prevail on the merits of its fraud claims against Doe defendants based on evidence that Thermo Energy has been the victim of a fraudulent phishing scheme. (Collard Decl. Exh. A-E 12). Thermo Energy has also shown that it is likely to suffer irreparable harm because of likelihood that absent a preliminary injunction as a result of the money that likely will be withdrawn from the accounts and dissipated before the entry of judgment. Wells Fargo Bank and its officers, employees, agents, contractors, and representatives, and all entities acting by or through them, including trustees, are enjoined from directly or indirectly doing or attempting to proceed with any transfer, disposal, removal, withdrawal or any other action whatsoever with respect to the monies deposited in Wells Fargo Bank accounts xxxxxx4046 and xxxxxx4826 until entry of final judgment in this action. Per CCP 529 Thermo Energy is required to post a bond in the amount of $10,000.00. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

Continued to February 11, 2019 by stipulation of all parties (email dated 1/23/19).

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
